What is artificial intelligence (AI) in finance?
================================================

 8 December 2023 

 Link copied 

[](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?url=https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/artificial-intelligence-finance&title=Artificial%20Intelligence%20in%20finance)[](https://www.facebook.com/share.php?u=https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/artificial-intelligence-finance)[](https://x.com/intent/tweet?text=Artificial%20Intelligence%20in%20finance&url=https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/artificial-intelligence-finance)

Authors
-------

[Matthew Finio](https://www.ibm.com/think/author/matthew-finio)
Content Writer

IBM Consulting

[Amanda Downie](https://www.ibm.com/content/adobe-cms/us/en/think/author/amanda-downie)
Inbound Content Lead, AI Productivity & IBM Consulting

What is AI in finance?
----------------------

Artificial intelligence (AI) in finance is the use of technology, including advanced algorithms and machine learning (ML), to analyze data, automate tasks and improve decision-making in the financial services industry.

[Artificial intelligence](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/artificial-intelligence)in finance refers to the application of a set of technologies, particularly [machine learning](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/machine-learning) algorithms, in the finance industry. This fintech enables financial services organizations to improve the efficiency, accuracy and speed of such tasks as data analytics, forecasting, investment management, risk management, fraud detection, customer service and more.

AI is modernizing the financial industry by automating traditionally manual banking processes, enabling a better understanding of financial markets and creating ways to engage customers that mimic human intelligence and interaction.

AI is revolutionizing how financial institutions operate and fueling startups. AI models run trades with remarkable speed and precision, taking advantage of real-time market data to unlock deeper insights and dictate where investments are made. By analyzing intricate patterns in transaction datasets, AI solutions allow financial organizations to improve [risk management](https://www.ibm.com/topics/risk-management), which includes security, fraud, anti-money laundering (AML), know your customer (KYC) and compliance initiatives.

AI is also changing the way financial organizations engage with customers, predicting their behavior and understanding their purchase preferences. This prediction enables more personalized interactions, faster and more accurate customer support, credit scoring refinements and innovative products and services.

Overall, the integration of AI in finance is creating a new era of data-driven decision-making, efficiency, security and customer experience in the financial sector.

![Image 1: Black woman working on laptop](https://www.ibm.com/content/dam/connectedassets-adobe-cms/worldwide-content/stock-assets/getty/image/photography/d3/03/pi-2778437.component.think-ad-xl.ts=1746031261570.jpg/content/experience-fragments/adobe-cms/us/en/site-v2/think-hub/article/_8_column_general_ad/blueprint---think-ad---xf---do-not-modify/thinkad-article-generalnewsletter/_jcr_content/root/think_ad_copy/image)

### Stay ahead of the latest tech news

Get weekly insights, research and expert views on AI, security, cloud and more in the Think Newsletter.

[Subscribe today](https://www.ibm.com/account/reg/signup?formid=news-urx-52954)

How is AI used in finance?
--------------------------

Here are some key areas where AI is commonly applied in the financial industry:

**Algorithmic trading:**AI can be used to develop trading algorithms that can analyze market trends and historical data to decide and perform trades faster than humans.

**Automation and efficiency:**AI can automate repetitive and time-consuming tasks, allowing financial institutions to process large amounts of data faster and more accurately.

**Competitive advantage:**AI can help financial institutions foster innovation and stay at the forefront of technology, which can give them a competitive edge.

**Compliance:**AI can automate monitoring and reporting requirements to ensure regulatory compliance

**Credit scoring:**AI can analyze various data, including social media activity and other online behavior, to assess customers’ creditworthiness and make more accurate credit decisions.

**Cost reduction:**By automating tasks, financial institutions can reduce manual labor, streamline workflows and improve operational efficiency, which can reduce costs.

**Customer service:**By answering questions and completing routine tasks 24/7, AI-powered personal assistants and [chatbots](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/chatbots) can reduce the need for human intervention, provide personalized customer service such as real-time credit approvals and offer consumers improved fraud protection and cybersecurity.

**Data analysis:**AI can analyze massive amounts of data and extract insights and trends that would be difficult for human data scientists to detect, enabling more informed decision-making and a deeper understanding of market behavior.

**Fraud detection:**AI algorithms can prevent financial crime, such as fraud and cyberattacks, by identifying unusual patterns in financial transactions. This approach helps improve security in activities such as online banking and credit card transactions.

**Loan processing:**AI can better predict and assess loan risks, and streamline the process and approvals for borrowers by automating tasks such as risk assessment, credit scoring and document verification.

**Personal finances:**AI tools can help people manage their personal finances by analyzing goals, spending patterns and risk tolerance to develop budgeting advice and savings strategies.

**Portfolio management:**AI can analyze market conditions and economic indicators to help investors make better decisions and optimize their portfolios.

**Predictive analytics:**AI can enable predictive modeling, which can help financial organizations anticipate market trends, potential risks and customer behavior.

**Risk management:**AI can analyze data to help financial organizations assess and manage risks more effectively and create a more secure and stable financial environment.

**Sentiment analysis:**AI can analyze news sources, social media and other information to gauge market sentiment, which can help predict market trends and influence decision-making.

 AI Academy 

### Put AI to work for finance

Generative AI is completely revolutionizing the role of finance. Learn how the adoption of AI is helping CFOs and finance teams find new ways of making the seemingly impossible, possible.

[Go to episode](https://www.ibm.com/think/videos/ai-academy/ai-finance)

Key stakeholders of AI in finance
---------------------------------

A diverse set of stakeholders implement, operate, regulate and use AI technologies in the financial sector. These participants include:

**Auditors and internal control teams:**Responsible for assessing the effectiveness of AI systems, these individuals and groups conduct audits to identify potential issues and risks and ensure efficiency, accuracy and compliance.

**Chief information officers (CIOs) and chief technology officers (CTOs):**As overseers of the organization’s technical infrastructure, CIOs and CTOs make key decisions regarding AI implementation, usage and security.

**Customers:**A positive user experience with AI-driven apps is necessary for customers and end users to have confidence and trust in the financial organization.

**Developers:**AI developers are responsible for designing and implementing AI systems into the organization, and ensuring their accuracy and effectiveness.

**Ethics and diversity officers:**Organizations task these individuals with guarding against bias, ensuring fairness and inclusivity in the use of AI.

**Executives**: Top executives and the board of directors make strategic decisions regarding the implementation and use of AI initiatives and their proper management.

**Financial organizations**: Banks, investment firms and other financial institutions implement AI to increase the effectiveness of fraud detection, risk management, underwriting, investment strategies and customer service.

**Legal teams:**These teams work with regulators to ensure that AI applications comply with relevant laws and industry regulations.

**Risk management teams:**As AI is often used for assessing and mitigating risk in financial organizations, these teams monitor the effectiveness of the AI systems.

Governance of AI in finance
---------------------------

The use of AI in finance requires monitoring to ensure proper use and minimal risk. Proactive governance can drive responsible, ethical and transparent AI usage, which is critical as financial institutions handle vast amounts of sensitive data.

Guardrails to ensure ethics, regulatory compliance, transparency and explainability—so that stakeholders understand the decisions made by the financial institution—are essential in order to balance the benefits of AI with responsible and accountable use. By establishing oversight and clear rules regarding its application, AI can continue to evolve as a trusted, powerful tool in the financial industry.

Use cases of AI in finance
--------------------------

Various types of financial institutions leverage AI to improve efficiency, decision-making and user experience (UX). Some examples of AI in finance include:

**Customer service:**[Conversational AI](https://research.ibm.com/topics/conversational-ai) and [natural language processing](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/natural-language-processing) (NLP) power chatbots that allow banking customers to access account information quickly and efficiently, 24/7.

**Cyberattack prevention:**AI can use [data science](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/data-science) to analyze patterns and trends and alert companies to unusual activity.

**Financial planning:**Robo-advisors use sophisticated algorithms to provide affordable, personalized investment advice based on customers’ goals, risk tolerance and market conditions.

**Fraud detection and prevention:**Deep learning can be used to analyze customers’ buying behavior and trigger an alert when unusual spending patterns are identified.

**Loan eligibility:**Lenders are managing risk by deploying AI neural networks to quickly analyze data to determine customers’ creditworthiness.

**Trading:**Investment firms use AI for algorithmic trading—trades made at high-speed based on real-time data and market trends.

AI in Financial Planning: The CFO’s Guide to Strategic Decision-Making
======================================================================

![Image 4](https://www.mindbridge.ai/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/AI-in-Financial-Planning-Blog.png)

[January 29, 2025](https://www.mindbridge.ai/blog/2025/01/)

### Share:

![Image 5: Picture of MindBridge](https://www.mindbridge.ai/wp-content/uploads/2022/04/MB-icon1-300x300.png)

#### MindBridge

The financial landscape is evolving rapidly, and CFOs are at the forefront of this transformation. **AI in financial planning** is no longer a futuristic concept—it’s a competitive advantage that enhances decision-making, risk management, and strategic execution.

AI-powered tools enable finance professionals to analyze complex financial flows in real time, surface hidden risks, and optimize planning with predictive intelligence. From continuous risk assessment to compliance automation and smarter investment strategies, AI is redefining how financial leaders operate.

This article explores the critical role of AI in financial planning, key technologies shaping the future, real-world applications, and how CFOs can leverage AI for smarter, data-driven decision-making.

The Current State of AI in Financial Planning
---------------------------------------------

The adoption of AI in financial planning has grown exponentially in recent years. The use of AI, big data, and blockchain to improve investment performance is widespread among asset managers, with over 90% already employing these tools, according to [PwC’s 2023 Global Asset & Wealth Management Survey](https://www.pwc.com/gx/en/news-room/press-releases/2023/pwc-2023-global-asset-and-wealth-management-survey.html).

The same survey projects a more than doubling of assets under robo-advisor management, from US$2.5 trillion in 2022 to US$5.9 trillion by 2027, showcasing the increasing use of AI in financial advising.

Plus, 60% of business and risk leaders view Generative AI as an opportunity for their businesses, indicating a positive outlook toward AI integration. This is a great reflection of [the state of AI in the office of the CFO](https://www.mindbridge.ai/blog/the-state-of-ai-in-the-office-of-the-cfo) and how it continues to shape the decision-making processes and resource allocation.

[Key takeaways on AI in finance from the Gartner CFO](https://www.mindbridge.ai/blog/key-takeaways-on-ai-in-finance-from-the-gartner-cfo-conference-2024/) show that while AI is top of mind, the journey is in its early phases for most businesses, with many still pondering the costs and ROI of such an investment.

AI has transitioned from a futuristic concept to a tangible tool that can help drive decisions in wealth management, investment strategies, and client advisory services.

Key Technologies Powering AI in Financial Planning
--------------------------------------------------

*   **Machine learning (ML):** Used for predictive modeling, portfolio optimization, and trend analysis.

*   **Natural language processing (NLP):** Enhances client communication through chatbots and sentiment analysis.

*   **Large language models (LLMs):** Tools like GPT-4 help create financial forecasts, draft client reports, and answer complex queries.

*   **Generative AI:** Enables the creation of personalized strategies and visual content for client presentations.

How AI Is Transforming Financial Planning
-----------------------------------------

AI can be used for financial planning in various ways. From offering personalized financial advice to improved risk management, AI-powered auditing in the digital age can make organizations more effective and reduce financial risks. Here are its main applications.

### **Personalized Financial Advice**

Have you ever wanted to log onto a platform that can review your financial history, project future needs, and recommend a personalized investment strategy all within minutes? AI can make such scenarios possible through what are typically known as robo-advisors.

These are online platforms that use algorithms to provide financial advice and manage investments. They collect information about your goals, income, and risk tolerance, then create and monitor a personalized investment plan for you automatically.

[Well-known robo-advisors](https://www.forbes.com/advisor/investing/best-robo-advisors/) include Betterment and Wealthfront and use algorithms to analyze individual client goals, risk tolerance, and financial data, offering tailored investment strategies. This AI-powered customization ensures that clients receive advice aligned with their unique needs, often at a fraction of traditional advisory costs.

### **Data Analysis and Insights**

The sheer volume of financial data is overwhelming for human advisors, but AI excels at processing and interpreting it. Machine learning models identify market trends, customer behavior patterns, and predictive analytics to help financial advisors make informed decisions.

AI-driven decision intelligence continuously analyzes financial flows, surfacing hidden risks and opportunities in real time. By leveraging machine learning and anomaly detection, firms can make more confident, proactive financial decisions.

### **Risk Assessment and Management**

Risk evaluation is critical in financial planning, and AI brings unparalleled precision to the process. AI algorithms analyze historical performance, market conditions, and economic indicators, offering a clear portfolio assessment with ease.

Plus, real-time monitoring systems use AI to track market movements, economic indicators, and portfolio performance as they happen. This allows financial advisors and clients to receive immediate alerts about significant changes, such as sudden market downturns or emerging risks in specific investments.

With this capability, they can act quickly to mitigate potential losses or capitalize on opportunities, ensuring more agile and informed decision-making.

The table below provides a quick comparison between AI-powered risk assessment and traditional risk assessment.

**Feature****Traditional risk assessment****AI-powered risk assessment**
Data processing speed Manual and time-consuming Automated and fast
Predictive accuracy Relies on historical data Incorporates real-time insights
Customization Limited Highly tailored

Benefits of AI in Financial Planning
------------------------------------

Many people still have doubts about using AI in general, and even more so in sensitive areas like financial planning. But when used correctly, AI can provide many benefits, such as improving accuracy and reducing costs.

### **Improved Efficiency and Accuracy**

AI eliminates many manual processes, reducing errors and increasing accuracy in financial forecasting and reporting. Wealth management professionals can now handle larger client bases without compromising service quality.

### **Enhanced Client Experience and Engagement**

Clients expect personalized, data-driven advice. Offering each one of them the attention they need can be challenging when working with a large client base.

With AI, you can offer clients 24/7 availability through robo-advisors and chatbots. They may not always be able to solve all queries, but they can provide answers to common questions, improving the client’s experience and reducing their wait times.

Plus, AI also comes with interactive tools for financial goal tracking and portfolio visualization, which make financial planning more engaging and accessible. They allow clients to see real-time updates on their progress, experiment with different scenarios, and better understand complex financial concepts through visuals, fostering greater confidence and involvement in their financial journey.

### **Cost Reduction and Scalability**

AI tools help financial advisors and firms scale operations while keeping costs low. For instance, in the financial services sector, AI applications have achieved a [45% reduction in case resolution time](https://ttms.com/financial-forecasting-and-planning-with-ai/). This efficiency not only cuts costs but also enhances customer retention by approximately 35%. By automating repetitive tasks like data entry and portfolio rebalancing, financial professionals can focus on higher-value activities.

Challenges and Considerations
-----------------------------

Despite all its benefits, AI is not without challenges. You’ll need to pay special attention to regulatory compliance, ensure your algorithms don’t insert biases, and find the right balance between AI use and human intervention.

### **Ethical Concerns and Bias**

AI algorithms are only as unbiased as the data they are trained on. Companies must ensure transparency and fairness to avoid discriminatory outcomes. Address algorithmic bias and make sure the AI systems you use clearly show how and why they make decisions.

### **Regulatory Compliance**

The regulatory landscape for AI in finance is still unclear. In the EU, there is the [EU AI Act](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1689/oj), which came into force on August 1st, 2024, and established a comprehensive framework to regulate AI systems based on their potential risks. In the US, the [AI Bill of Rights](https://www.whitehouse.gov/ostp/ai-bill-of-rights/) offers a non-binding framework that aims to ensure the ethical development and deployment of AI.

Firms must navigate complex data privacy laws like GDPR and CCPA while ensuring **financial data integrity and audit compliance.** AI-powered anomaly detection strengthens oversight, **reducing regulatory risks and ensuring financial transparency.**Robust data encryption and secure storage practices are non-negotiable.

### **Human vs. AI: Finding the Right Balance**

AI **amplifies human expertise** by providing real-time, data-driven insights. While automation enhances efficiency, strategic decision-making still relies on human intelligence. AI ensures financial professionals can **spend less time on manual tasks and more time delivering high-value advisory services.**Emotional intelligence, trust-building, and strategic thinking are areas where human advisors shine. To be successful, we need to carefully combine AI’s computational power with human expertise.

The Future of AI in Financial Planning: Trends and Predictions
--------------------------------------------------------------

[AI adoption in finance surged](https://artsmart.ai/blog/ai-in-finance-statistics-trends/) from 45% in 2022 to an expected 85% by 2025, with 60% of companies using AI across multiple business areas.

Advancements in natural language processing and conversational AI improve the accessibility of financial advice. Advisors can use natural language processing (NLP) tools to draft personalized client communications and create detailed financial reports, streamlining processes and improving client engagement.

There are also various other emerging technologies that, together with AI, can boost results. One such example is blockchain, which facilitates secure and transparent financial transactions. The Internet of Things (IoT) also enables real-time data collection for informed financial decision-making.

AI-powered decision intelligence is no longer optional—it’s essential for financial professionals looking to navigate risk, enhance compliance, and optimize decision-making. By integrating AI-driven insights, firms can ensure financial integrity, identify hidden risks, and empower strategic planning with confidence.

How to Successfully Implement AI in Financial Planning
------------------------------------------------------

AI can change the way you approach financial planning, but how to implement it in your company?

Start by assessing AI readiness. Evaluate current processes and identify areas where you can integrate AI. Conduct a cost-benefit analysis to determine ROI, and finally, review data privacy frameworks to ensure you’ll be able to comply with applicable regulations.

Once you’ve determined your company is ready and have decided on the tools you’ll use, it’s best to start small with a pilot program. Focus on integration with your existing tools. Ideally, you want to avoid having to change all the software in the company for the sake of AI adoption. Most AI vendors will offer tailored solutions to guarantee the best outcome for each business, so work with them to find the best strategy for you.

Remember to offer training and upskilling for financial professionals who need to learn to[navigate AI fundamentals in](https://www.mindbridge.ai/docs/library/MindBridge-Navigating-AI-Fundamentals-for-Accounting-and-Finance-02082024.pdf?utm_campaign=2024%20Matthias%20Blogs&utm_source=website&utm_medium=blog%20post&utm_term=State%20of%20AI%20in%20the%20office%20of%20the%20CFO&utm_content=Navigating-AI-fundamentals-for-accounting-and-finance)[accounting](https://www.mindbridge.ai/docs/library/MindBridge-Navigating-AI-Fundamentals-for-Accounting-and-Finance-02082024.pdf?utm_campaign=2024%20Matthias%20Blogs&utm_source=website&utm_medium=blog%20post&utm_term=State%20of%20AI%20in%20the%20office%20of%20the%20CFO&utm_content=Navigating-AI-fundamentals-for-accounting-and-finance). Training programs can include data analysis and interpretation, effective use of AI tools, and ethical considerations when working with AI.

**Ready to Future-Proof Your Financial Strategy with AI?**
----------------------------------------------------------

AI in financial planning isn’t just an innovation—it’s a necessity for CFOs and financial leaders looking to enhance risk management, compliance, and strategic decision-making. Whether you’re looking to optimize financial workflows, improve forecasting accuracy, or gain deeper insights into financial data, AI-powered solutions can give your business a competitive edge.

**Take the next step:**

*   Explore AI-powered financial planning tools that fit your firm’s needs
*   Assess your AI readiness and identify key areas for AI integration
*   Stay ahead of the curve with industry-leading insights on AI in finance

FAQs
----

#### **1. How does AI improve the accuracy of financial planning?**

AI analyzes vast datasets and provides real-time insights, significantly reducing errors in forecasting and reporting.

#### **2. Can AI completely replace human financial advisors?**

No, AI complements human advisors by automating repetitive tasks and providing data-driven insights, allowing professionals to focus on relationship-building and strategic planning.

#### **3. What are the potential risks of relying on AI for financial planning?**

Key risks include algorithmic bias, lack of transparency, privacy, and cybersecurity threats.

#### **4. How does AI enhance the personalization of financial advice?**

AI tailors financial plans by analyzing individual client data, goals, and preferences, creating customized investment strategies.

#### **5. What skills do financial professionals need to work effectively with AI tools?**

Financial advisors should focus on data interpretation, ethical AI use, and integrating AI insights into client interactions.

#### **6. How does AI help in managing and mitigating investment risks?**

AI algorithms evaluate risks in real time, providing proactive alerts and deeper insights into portfolio vulnerabilities.

#### **7. What regulatory considerations should firms be aware of when implementing AI in financial planning?**

Firms must comply with data privacy laws, ensure algorithmic transparency, and adhere to financial industry regulations.

#### **8. How can small financial advisory firms leverage AI technology?**

Small firms can adopt cost-effective AI tools for tasks like client onboarding, risk assessment, and automated reporting. AI-powered tools can also provide personalized investment recommendations and predictive analytics, helping advisors deliver tailored client solutions.
